title: HOMEPLATE - 211info
text: HOMEPLATE THURSDAY NIGHT DROP IN CENTER HOMEPLATE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a weekly drop-in center where youth can access food, clothing, showers, and hygiene products and receive referrals for other resources. Crafts, sports, games, and other activities may also be held at the drop-in centers. Outreach workers are also available to meet youth anywhere in Washington County to discuss resources. Operates education and employment programs with one-on-one support for job search, application assistance, skills training, resume and cover letter creation, interview coaching, school reconnection, navigating financial aid, enrolling in college or trade schools, and life skills. 494 E Main Street Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Youth ages 12-24 who are experiencing homelessness or at risk of homelessness. Youth may access services until their 25th birthday. Hours: Thursday 6pm-8pm Email (503) 608-7252 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 608-7252 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Walk in. Youth are asked to provide their name and age only at the drop-in center. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Washington County SITE HOURS: 6pm-8pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: SW corner of 5th and Main in the First Congregational UCC Church basement. The TriMet MAX blue line and bus lines 33, 46, 47, 48, and 57 all stop at the Hillsboro Central/SE 3rd Ave Transit Center at 3rd and Washington. From there, walk 2 blocks east to 5th and 1 block north to Main. Services: Community Meals * Homeless Youth General Recreational Activities/Sports * Homeless Youth Homeless Drop In Centers * Youth Issues Job Finding Assistance Personal/Grooming Supplies * Homeless Youth Prejob Guidance Public Showers/Baths * Homeless Youth Specialized Information and Referral * Homelessness Issues Street Outreach Programs * Homeless Youth If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
